title: Cross-chain borrow trusts unverified collateral snapshot from source chain
reason: Duplicate of existing F-002 (same stale-collateral TOCTOU root cause and exploit path).

title: Cross-chain liquidation allows seize without confirmed repayment on destination chain
reason: Duplicate of existing F-007 (seize-before-repay ordering flaw).

title: Gas griefing in claimLend via unbounded iterator arrays
reason: Primarily caller self-grief: attacker supplies arrays and pays gas; does not create a realistic protocol-level exploit by itself.

title: Protocol reward update lacks cap and access control beyond owner
reason: Assumes compromise/malicious behavior of already-authorized contracts; not a distinct permissionless vulnerability.

title: LayerZero message options use hardcoded gas limit that may be insufficient
reason: Too speculative/configuration-dependent as presented; no concrete, reproducible protocol-harm path established from current code alone.

title: Borrow index validation missing for edge case in liquidation calculation
reason: Relies on corrupted/impossible-by-normal-flow state (`storedBorrowIndex == 0`) rather than a realistic reachable condition.

title: Cross-chain repay state update uses inconsistent index source
reason: Using current borrow index on partial repay is expected debt normalization behavior; no concrete accounting break demonstrated.

title: Cross-chain borrow market-entry check is self-fulfilling and can skip actual Comptroller entry
reason: Logic mismatch exists, but exploit impact is not clearly protocol-level (primarily operational/availability behavior and may only cause caller-side borrow failure).

title: Liquidation execute handler can hard-revert on unchecked collateral subtraction
reason: Underflow revert is possible for bad liquidation parameters, but this is mainly per-call failure by caller-selected inputs and not shown as a persistent protocol-level exploit independent of already-tracked issues.

title: Same-chain liquidation path can revert by division-by-zero when no local borrow index exists
reason: Occurs when attempting same-chain liquidation without same-chain borrow state; this is an invalid-path revert with limited security impact, not a realistic exploitable protocol vulnerability.
